channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gwadar Port Strengthens Role as Regional Trade Hub Amid Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Gwadar Port is rapidly emerging as a crucial trade and logistics hub for Pakistan and the wider region. According to Gwadar Port Authority Chairman Noorul Haq Baloch, the port’s strategic importance h...

item.title: Man City Beat Brentford 3-0 To Stay In Premier League Title Race
item.description: Manchester City still dey fight for Premier League title after dem hammer Brentford 3-0 for Etihad Stadium on Saturday. Jeremy Doku shine with one stunning goal wey break the stalemate, and Erling Haa...

item.title: Two Dead and Two Injured in Head-On Collision on Mount Emma Road, Littlerock
item.description: On Friday evening, a head-on collision on Mount Emma Road in Littlerock, near Palmdale, California, resulted in the deaths of two individuals and injuries to two others. Preliminary reports from the C...

item.title: NSW land tax dispute leaves family wineries facing unexpected bills
item.description: A growing dispute has emerged in New South Wales over how land tax rules are being applied to family-owned wineries, with several producers unexpectedly hit with tax bills. Revenue NSW has reassessed ...

item.title: Manchester City Renames Stadium to Support Charity During Brentford Game
item.description: Manchester City recently played their Premier League match against Brentford and won comfortably 3-0, but there was a notable change at their home ground. For this specific game, the Etihad Stadium wa...

item.title: Dickson Says NDC Growing Strong as Credible Opposition in Nigeria
item.description: Former Bayelsa State Governor and leader of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Seriake Dickson, don yan say multiparty democracy must survive for Naija. During the NDC National Convention wey hold...

item.title: Developers shift focus to smaller cities due to rising housing demand
item.description: Developers are increasingly turning their attention to smaller Australian cities such as Adelaide and Perth. The rising property prices in these locations are making new residential projects more fina...

item.title: Karen Nyamu’s Siaka Dance at Nairobi Event Draws Mixed Reactions Amid Ongoing Controversy
item.description: Nominated senator Karen Nyamu recently caught public attention after a video showed her dancing to Mejja’s popular track Siaka during a public event in Nairobi’s CBD. The event, held on May 8, was pri...

item.title: Charo Santos-Concio Impresses Fans at 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' Screening
item.description: Charo Santos-Concio, renowned for her elegance and influential presence in Philippine media, recently made a memorable appearance at the block screening of 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'. Channeling the ic...

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Caloy Weakens but Continues to Bring Rain to Eastern Visayas and Mindanao
item.description: Tropical Storm Caloy, internationally known as Hagupit, has weakened into a tropical depression as it moves west-northwest within the Philippine Area of Responsibility, according to PAGASA. As of the ...

item.title: Liberal Democrats secure council gains across England and Scotland while positioning as moderate alternative to Reform UK and Greens
item.description: Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey has described his party as offering a sensible alternative to the extremes of Reform UK and the Green Party, following local and devolved elections across the UK. ...

item.title: Multiple people injured in Florida boat explosion
item.description: A boat explosion in Florida has resulted in injuries to at least 15 individuals, according to initial reports. Authorities responded quickly to the incident, providing medical assistance and transport...

item.title: Waratahs’ forward struggles and defensive lapses exposed in loss to Highlanders
item.description: The Waratahs’ 31-26 loss to the Highlanders has become a defining example of their inconsistent Super Rugby season, particularly highlighting weaknesses in their forward pack and defensive execution. ...

item.title: Motorcyclist hospitalized after Surrey-Delta crash leaves bike suspended from traffic signal
item.description: A motorcyclist sustained serious but non-life-threatening injuries following a crash with a car near the Surrey-Delta border in British Columbia on Saturday afternoon. According to Delta police, the c...

item.title: Manitoba declares HIV a public health emergency amid rising cases
item.description: Manitoba has officially declared HIV a public health emergency, a move welcomed by health advocates and community leaders. This designation allows provincial authorities to allocate more resources tow...

item.title: Exploring Mother-Daughter Bonds Through Books
item.description: In this reflective piece, Sonya Dutta Choudhury shares a personal experience of exploring the complex relationship between mothers and daughters through literature. She describes a serene afternoon sp...

item.title: Rove McManus Talks Fans, Taskmaster, and Life in Showbiz
item.description: Rove McManus, former chat show host and current Taskmaster Australia contestant, sat down for a wide-ranging interview covering pop culture opinions, memorable fan encounters, and behind-the-scenes in...

item.title: Sheila Mutundi Returns to Kenya to Reunite with Family and Share Airline Plans
item.description: Sheila Mutundi, a Kenyan model based in Singapore, recently returned home and created excitement online after visiting her parents in Kenya. She shared photos highlighting the stark contrast between t...

item.title: Pakistan Commemorates One Year Since Marka-i-Haq Victory at GHQ
item.description: A formal ceremony is ongoing at Pakistan’s General Headquarters (GHQ) in Rawalpindi to mark the first anniversary of the nation’s victory in last year’s conflict with India, which has been officially ...

item.title: Constitutional Court to Rule on Legality of Parliament Rejecting Impeachment Report Against President Ramaphosa
item.description: On 8 May 2026, the Constitutional Court is set to rule on the legal question of whether Parliament acted within its powers when it declined to act on a Section 89 panel report that recommended an impe...
